description:
# Open question

The delivery/worktree plugin contract — the SIBLING plugin (NOT base balls, NOT tracker) that owns the deliverable **code worktree** end to end. bl-d66e (state-transitions) decided this plugin EXISTS and drew the boundary (core flips ball state on the `balls` branch; this plugin owns everything touching the project repo) but DEFERRED its actual contract. No other topic owns it. Until it's specced, `bl review` (resolved, bl-d66e) and `bl close` (open, bl-dd3e) both hand off concrete behavior to an unwritten plugin — which is why they read as vague.

## Why a plugin, not core (settled — do not re-litigate; see bl-2e26 "§ STATE TRANSITIONS")

Base balls operates only on the balls-state clone (`operating/`); it never opens the project repo, so "nothing on main" is structural. Splitting state-flip (core) from code-delivery (plugin) makes local-squash vs forge-gated just "which plugin is wired into `review.post`." A SIBLING of tracker (not bundled): local-worktrees-without-remote and remote-without-worktrees must be independently expressible.

## Scope — spec this plugin's contract

- HOOKS it wires and what each does:
  - `claim.post` — materialize the code worktree (`git -C <project-repo> worktree add <derived-path> -b work/<id>`), create-if-absent.
  - `review.post` — the delivery step: commit WIP, merge integration→work/<id> (conflicts surface here), squash work/<id>→integration, write `delivered_in`. Forge-gated variant pushes work/<id> + opens PR + creates the gate child instead, leaving `delivered_in` null.
  - `unclaim.post` / `drop.post` — release the worktree (drop-from-`open` = no-op by idempotency).
  - `close.post` — teardown. (DEFERRED to bl-dd3e / bl-close-remote-coupling: whether close also SHIPS code, given review.post already squashed. Resolve there; this topic specs the teardown mechanics close.post invokes.)
- DERIVED-PATH convention: the worktree path is a pure function of (binding, id) so balls can compute+print it (preserving "claim prints the path") with NO return channel (note 3). Pin the exact function + where the worktree physically lives (plugin XDG territory).
- CROSS-OP STATE: the plugin remembers the worktree across claim→review→close (three ops); id-keyed scratch under `plugins/<name>/`. Coordinate with bl-b0b6 (rollback) which is choosing the same scratch convention.
- ROLLBACK ORDERING: the squash is an un-pushable side-effect; the delivery plugin sorts LAST (highest NN-) in `review.post/` so nothing runs after it. Its `rollback` verb un-squashes (local `git reset`) / removes a freshly-created worktree. Defer the general rule to bl-b0b6; spec this plugin's specifics.
- TWO DELIVERY VARIANTS as two plugins: local-squash (default) vs forge-gated. The CURRENT-balls forge work is the reference implementation for the forge variant (bl-00ca forge-gated epic, bl-ac91 SPEC-forge-gated-delivery.md, bl-b017 deferred-squash, gate-child mechanics) — port its semantics onto this contract, don't reinvent.
- `delivered_in`: who writes it (local-squash plugin on review.post) vs who leaves it null (forge variant, resolved later from the merge tag).

## Settled inputs (from bl-d66e — carry, don't redecide)

Two-cwd reality (plugin acts on the project repo, ignoring its change-worktree cwd, like tracker on a remote); `binding.invocation_path` added to the §7 wire so the plugin can find the project repo; failure order = rollback plugins reverse, then discard change worktree (note 3).

## Relation

Informs bl-dd3e (close/drop — can't answer "does close ship code?" without this). Consumes bl-b0b6 (rollback) + bl-e8a5 (doctor: the plugin-owned worktree is a second drift class, likely a plugin-contributed doctor check). Sibling to bl-8452 (dispatcher) which defines how it's invoked.
title: Topic: delivery/worktree plugin contract (claim/review/close hooks)
